xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers package¶

Public API for E3EE layer modules.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.AbsorberPathAggregator(atom_dim, rbf_dim, geom_hidden_dim, scatter_dim, out_dim, cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: Module

3-body absorber-centred path aggregator for paths (absorber, j, k).

Consumes precomputed flat triplet scalars (r0j, r0k, rjk, cos(angle)) and per-path flat atom indices into the padded B * N node layout, together with path_batch for scatter-aggregating into the batch dimension.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • geom_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the geometry MLP.

  • scatter_dim (int) – Intermediate scatter feature dimension.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff used for the cosine envelope weights.

__init__(atom_dim, rbf_dim, geom_hidden_dim, scatter_dim, out_dim, cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize AbsorberPathAggregator.

forward(h_flat, z_flat, pair_elem_energy, e_feat, path_j, path_k, path_r0j, path_r0k, path_rjk, path_cosangle, path_batch, bsz)[source]¶

Aggregate path contributions into per-(sample, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • h_flat (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (flattened), shape (B*N, atom_dim).

  • z_flat (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (flattened), shape (B*N,).

  • pair_elem_energy (PairElementEnergyScattering) – Pre-built element-pair energy module.

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

  • path_j (Tensor) – Flat atom indices for leg j (into B*N), shape (P,).

  • path_k (Tensor) – Flat atom indices for leg k (into B*N), shape (P,).

  • path_r0j (Tensor) – Absorber-to-j distance in Angstrom, shape (P,).

  • path_r0k (Tensor) – Absorber-to-k distance in Angstrom, shape (P,).

  • path_rjk (Tensor) – j-to-k distance in Angstrom, shape (P,).

  • path_cosangle (Tensor) – Cosine of the j-absorber-k angle, shape (P,).

  • path_batch (Tensor) – Batch index per path (0..B-1), shape (P,).

  • bsz (int) – Batch size.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.CosineCutoff(cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Smooth cosine cutoff envelope.

Smoothly decays from 1 at r = 0 to 0 at r = cutoff.

Parameters:

cutoff (float) – Cutoff radius in Angstrom beyond which the envelope is zero.

__init__(cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize CosineCutoff.

forward(r)[source]¶

Evaluate cosine envelope.

Parameters:

r (Tensor) – Interatomic distances of arbitrary shape (...) in Angstrom.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Envelope values in [0, 1] with the same shape as r.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EnergyConditionedAbsorberBranch(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-dependent absorber branch based on invariant absorber features.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of the invariant absorber feature vector.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the MLP.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedAbsorberBranch.

forward(h_abs, e_feat)[source]¶

Compute absorber branch latent from absorber features and energy embedding.

Parameters:

  • h_abs (Tensor) – Absorber invariant features, shape (B, H).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EnergyConditionedAtomAttention(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned attention over invariant atomwise features.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of the invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension; must be divisible by n_heads.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radius of the attention neighborhood graph in Angstrom.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for the absorber->atom distance.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • n_heads (int) – Number of attention heads.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedAtomAttention.

forward(h, z, mask, e_feat, absorber_index, att_dst, att_dist)[source]¶

Compute energy-conditioned attention over atoms and return latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask (encoder scope), shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber index per sample, shape (B,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N (attention scope), shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom distances in Angstrom, shape (E_att,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EnergyConditionedAtomConvolution(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Invariant SchNet/PaiNN-style convolution producing a per-(absorber, energy) latent.

For each sample’s absorber:

m_{abs<-j} = MLP(h_j, z_j, RBF(dist), is_abs) # SchNet message m_{abs<-j} *= cos_envelope(dist) [optional] m_{abs<-j} *= sigmoid(MLP(h_abs, h_j, RBF)) # PaiNN gate m_abs = sum_j (j in att-graph) # sum aggregation out_abs[e] = MLP(EnergyMod_scalar(m_abs, e_feat)) # energy mod

Energy conditioning runs after aggregation, so per-edge tensors carry no nE axis (large activation-memory saving).

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radius of the attention neighborhood graph in Angstrom.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for the absorber->atom distance.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style learned scalar edge gate.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedAtomConvolution.

forward(h, z, mask, e_feat, absorber_index, att_dst, att_dist)[source]¶

Compute invariant convolution branch latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber index per sample, shape (B,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N (absorber’s neighbors), shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom distances in Angstrom, shape (E_att,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EnergyConditionedEquivariantAbsorberHead(irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Late equivariant absorber head.

Applies energy-conditioned irrep-wise modulation to the absorber equivariant feature, converts to invariants, then projects.

Parameters:

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the absorber equivariant features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension used in the modulation and output MLPs.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

__init__(irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedEquivariantAbsorberHead.

forward(h_abs_full, e_feat)[source]¶

Extract invariants from absorber equivariant features and project to latent.

Parameters:

  • h_abs_full (Tensor) – Equivariant absorber features, shape (B, D).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomAttention(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Equivariant counterpart of EnergyConditionedAtomAttention.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features (used for scoring).

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the full equivariant atom features (TP input).

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension; must be divisible by n_heads.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radius of the attention neighborhood graph in Angstrom.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for bond directions.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Target irreps of the equivariant values (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o").

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for the absorber->atom distance.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • n_heads (int) – Number of attention heads used for invariant scoring.

__init__(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomAttention.

forward(h, h_full, z, mask, e_feat, absorber_index, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec)[source]¶

Compute equivariant energy-conditioned attention over atoms and return latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (used for scoring), shape (B, N, H).

  • h_full (Tensor) – Full equivariant atom features (TP input for values), shape (B, N, irreps_node.dim).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber index per sample, shape (B,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom distances in Angstrom, shape (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom displacement vectors in Angstrom, shape (E_att, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomConvolution(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

NequIP/MACE-style equivariant convolution producing a per-(absorber, energy) latent.

For each sample’s absorber:

sh_j = Y(u_{abs->j}) v_{abs<-j} = TP(h_full[j], sh_j; W(z_j, RBF, is_abs)) # NequIP message v_{abs<-j} *= cos_envelope(dist) [optional] v_{abs<-j} *= sigmoid(MLP(h_abs, h_j, RBF)) # PaiNN gate v_abs = sum_j v_{abs<-j} # sum agg v_{abs,e} = EnergyIrrepModulation(v_abs, e_feat) # energy mod out = MLP(invariants(v_{abs,e}))

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the full equivariant atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radius of the attention neighborhood graph in Angstrom.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for bond directions.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Target irreps of the equivariant message (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o").

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for the absorber->atom distance.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style learned scalar edge gate.

__init__(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomConvolution.

forward(h, h_full, z, mask, e_feat, absorber_index, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec)[source]¶

Apply equivariant convolution and return per-(absorber, energy) latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (B, N, H).

  • h_full (Tensor) – Full equivariant atom features (B, N, irreps_node.dim).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF embedding (nE, e_dim).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber index per sample (B,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N for the attention neighborhood (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom distances (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom displacement vectors (E_att, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EnergyIrrepModulation(irreps, e_dim, hidden_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned scalar modulation of each irrep copy, preserving equivariance.

A scalar gate per irrep copy is predicted from the energy embedding and applied multiplicatively to all dim-dimensional components of that copy. Because a scalar multiplier commutes with rotations, equivariance is preserved.

Parameters:

  • irreps (Irreps) – The irreps layout of the features to be modulated.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding (MLP input).

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the gate MLP.

__init__(irreps, e_dim, hidden_dim)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyIrrepModulation.

forward(x, e_feat)[source]¶

Apply per-copy scalar energy modulation to equivariant features.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Equivariant features, shape (B, D).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Modulated equivariant features of shape (B, nE, D).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EnergyRBFEmbedding(e_min, e_max, n_rbf)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Gaussian RBF embedding for scalar energy grid indices.

Parameters:

  • e_min (float) – Minimum grid index (left edge of RBF center grid).

  • e_max (float) – Maximum grid index (right edge of RBF center grid).

  • n_rbf (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

__init__(e_min, e_max, n_rbf)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyRBFEmbedding.

forward(energies)[source]¶

Embed energy grid indices as Gaussian RBF features.

Parameters:

energies (Tensor) – Energy grid indices of shape (nE,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

RBF features of shape (nE, n_rbf).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EquivariantAtomEncoder(max_z, cutoff, num_interactions, rbf_dim, lmax, node_attr_dim, hidden_dim, irreps_node, irreps_message, residual_scale_init)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Equivariant atom encoder with spherical harmonics message passing.

Parameters:

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number (inclusive).

  • cutoff (float) – Message-passing cutoff radius in Angstrom.

  • num_interactions (int) – Number of equivariant interaction blocks.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF basis functions for edge distances.

  • lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order.

  • node_attr_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic-number embeddings.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of radial weight MLPs.

  • irreps_node (str) – Output irreps of each interaction block, given as an e3nn irreps string (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o+8x2e").

  • irreps_message (str) – Intermediate irreps used for the tensor-product message before the gated non-linearity.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial value of the learnable residual scale parameter in each interaction block.

__init__(max_z, cutoff, num_interactions, rbf_dim, lmax, node_attr_dim, hidden_dim, irreps_node, irreps_message, residual_scale_init)[source]¶

Initialize EquivariantAtomEncoder.

forward(z, mask, absorber_index, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_weight, edge_vec)[source]¶

Encode atoms into equivariant features via multi-layer message passing.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (int64), shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber atom index per sample (0..N-1), shape (B,).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Source flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Destination flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Edge lengths in Angstrom (PBC-correct), shape (E,).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – Edge displacement vectors in Angstrom (PBC-correct), shape (E, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Equivariant atom features of shape (B, N, irreps_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.EquivariantInteractionBlock(irreps_node, irreps_sh, irreps_message, rbf_dim, radial_hidden_dim, cutoff, residual_scale_init=0.1)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Single equivariant message-passing interaction block using e3nn tensor products with gating.

Parameters:

  • irreps_node (str) – Irreps of the per-atom node features (input and output).

  • irreps_sh (str) – Irreps of the spherical harmonics on each edge.

  • irreps_message (str) – Intermediate irreps of the message before gating.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF features encoding edge lengths.

  • radial_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the radial weight MLP.

  • cutoff (float) – Smooth cutoff radius; edges beyond this have zero weight.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial value for the learnable residual scale.

__init__(irreps_node, irreps_sh, irreps_message, rbf_dim, radial_hidden_dim, cutoff, residual_scale_init=0.1)[source]¶

Initialize EquivariantInteractionBlock.

forward(x, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_sh, edge_rbf, edge_len)[source]¶

Apply one equivariant message-passing step.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Node features of shape (B*N, irreps_node.dim).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Source flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Destination flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_sh (Tensor) – Spherical harmonics on each edge, shape (E, irreps_sh.dim).

  • edge_rbf (Tensor) – RBF-encoded edge lengths, shape (E, rbf_dim).

  • edge_len (Tensor) – Raw edge lengths in Angstrom, shape (E,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated node features of shape (B*N, irreps_node.dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.GaussianRBF(start, stop, n_rbf, gamma=None)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Gaussian radial basis function expansion.

Parameters:

  • start (float) – Left edge of the RBF center grid.

  • stop (float) – Right edge of the RBF center grid.

  • n_rbf (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • gamma (float | None) – Width parameter 1 / sigma^2. Defaults to 1 / delta^2 where delta is the uniform spacing between centers.

__init__(start, stop, n_rbf, gamma=None)[source]¶

Initialize GaussianRBF.

forward(x)[source]¶

Expand scalar inputs into Gaussian RBF features.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input scalars of arbitrary leading shape (...).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

RBF features of shape (..., n_rbf).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.GatedBranchFusion(branch_dims, fused_dim, cond_dim, hidden_dim, use_softmax=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned gated fusion of active branch latents.

Each active branch is projected to fused_dim and combined with a learned soft gate predicted from the projected branch values, their magnitudes, and a conditioning feature such as the energy embedding.

Parameters:

  • branch_dims (list[int]) – Feature dimensions of the active branch tensors.

  • fused_dim (int) – Common latent dimension used for gated summation.

  • cond_dim (int) – Dimension of the conditioning features.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the gate and output MLPs.

  • use_softmax (bool) – If True, normalize gates with softmax. Otherwise, use sigmoid gates normalized to sum to one.

__init__(branch_dims, fused_dim, cond_dim, hidden_dim, use_softmax=True)[source]¶

Initialize GatedBranchFusion.

reset_gate_logits()[source]¶

Initialize the final gate layer to produce uniform gates.

Return type:

None

forward(branches, cond_feat, return_gates=False)[source]¶

Fuse active branch tensors.

Parameters:

  • branches (list[Tensor]) – Active branch tensors with matching leading dimensions. Each tensor has shape (..., branch_dim_i).

  • cond_feat (Tensor) – Conditioning features of shape (..., cond_dim) or any trailing-leading shape broadcastable to the branches, such as (nE, cond_dim) for branch tensors shaped (B, nE, D).

  • return_gates (bool) – If True, return the normalized gate weights along with the fused tensor.

Return type:

Tensor | tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

Returns:

Fused tensor of shape (..., fused_dim). If return_gates is True, returns (fused, gates) where gates has shape (..., n_branches).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.IrrepNorm(irreps, eps=1e-08, affine=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Irrep-respecting normalization.

For l = 0 blocks the multiplicity channels are mean/variance normalized. For l > 0 blocks each irrep copy is RMS-normalized across its irrep dimension.

Parameters:

  • irreps (Irreps) – The irreps specification that describes the feature layout.

  • eps (float) – Small constant added to denominators for numerical stability.

  • affine (bool) – If True, learn per-channel scale and bias parameters.

__init__(irreps, eps=1e-08, affine=True)[source]¶

Initialize IrrepNorm.

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply irrep-respecting normalization.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input features of shape (..., irreps.dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Normalized features with the same shape as x.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.MLP(in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim, n_layers=2, dropout=0.0, layer_norm=False)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Simple multi-layer perceptron with SiLU activations.

Parameters:

  • in_dim (int) – Size of the input features.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Size of each hidden layer.

  • out_dim (int) – Size of the output features.

  • n_layers (int) – Total number of linear layers (including the output layer).

  • dropout (float) – Dropout probability applied after each hidden activation. Set to 0.0 to disable.

  • layer_norm (bool) – If True, apply LayerNorm after each hidden linear layer.

__init__(in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim, n_layers=2, dropout=0.0, layer_norm=False)[source]¶

Initialize MLP.

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply the MLP.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (..., in_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (..., out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.PairElementEnergyScattering(max_z, z_emb_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned element-pair scattering features.

Operates on flat per-path tensors (P, ...) rather than padded (B, n_paths, ...).

Parameters:

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the output MLP.

  • out_dim (int) – Output feature dimension per path.

__init__(max_z, z_emb_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize PairElementEnergyScattering.

forward(z_j, z_k, e_feat)[source]¶

Compute per-path element-pair energy features.

Parameters:

  • z_j (Tensor) – Atomic numbers of the j leg, shape (P,).

  • z_k (Tensor) – Atomic numbers of the k leg, shape (P,).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-path energy features of shape (P, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.RadialMLP(in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Two-hidden-layer MLP for radial (TP) weight prediction.

Parameters:

  • in_dim (int) – Input size (typically RBF dimension plus optional extras).

  • hidden_dim (int) – Size of each hidden layer.

  • out_dim (int) – Output size (typically the number of TP weights).

__init__(in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize RadialMLP.

forward(x)[source]¶

Predict radial weights.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input features of shape (..., in_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Predicted weights of shape (..., out_dim).

Submodules¶

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.atom_encoder module¶

Equivariant atom encoder with spherical-harmonics message passing for E3EE.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.atom_encoder.EquivariantAtomEncoder(max_z, cutoff, num_interactions, rbf_dim, lmax, node_attr_dim, hidden_dim, irreps_node, irreps_message, residual_scale_init)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Equivariant atom encoder with spherical harmonics message passing.

Parameters:

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number (inclusive).

  • cutoff (float) – Message-passing cutoff radius in Angstrom.

  • num_interactions (int) – Number of equivariant interaction blocks.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF basis functions for edge distances.

  • lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order.

  • node_attr_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic-number embeddings.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of radial weight MLPs.

  • irreps_node (str) – Output irreps of each interaction block, given as an e3nn irreps string (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o+8x2e").

  • irreps_message (str) – Intermediate irreps used for the tensor-product message before the gated non-linearity.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial value of the learnable residual scale parameter in each interaction block.

__init__(max_z, cutoff, num_interactions, rbf_dim, lmax, node_attr_dim, hidden_dim, irreps_node, irreps_message, residual_scale_init)[source]¶

Initialize EquivariantAtomEncoder.

forward(z, mask, absorber_index, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_weight, edge_vec)[source]¶

Encode atoms into equivariant features via multi-layer message passing.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (int64), shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber atom index per sample (0..N-1), shape (B,).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Source flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Destination flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Edge lengths in Angstrom (PBC-correct), shape (E,).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – Edge displacement vectors in Angstrom (PBC-correct), shape (E, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Equivariant atom features of shape (B, N, irreps_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.basic module¶

Primitive building blocks (RBF, cutoffs, MLP, IrrepNorm) shared across E3EE layers.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.basic.GaussianRBF(start, stop, n_rbf, gamma=None)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Gaussian radial basis function expansion.

Parameters:

  • start (float) – Left edge of the RBF center grid.

  • stop (float) – Right edge of the RBF center grid.

  • n_rbf (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • gamma (float | None) – Width parameter 1 / sigma^2. Defaults to 1 / delta^2 where delta is the uniform spacing between centers.

__init__(start, stop, n_rbf, gamma=None)[source]¶

Initialize GaussianRBF.

forward(x)[source]¶

Expand scalar inputs into Gaussian RBF features.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input scalars of arbitrary leading shape (...).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

RBF features of shape (..., n_rbf).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.basic.CosineCutoff(cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Smooth cosine cutoff envelope.

Smoothly decays from 1 at r = 0 to 0 at r = cutoff.

Parameters:

cutoff (float) – Cutoff radius in Angstrom beyond which the envelope is zero.

__init__(cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize CosineCutoff.

forward(r)[source]¶

Evaluate cosine envelope.

Parameters:

r (Tensor) – Interatomic distances of arbitrary shape (...) in Angstrom.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Envelope values in [0, 1] with the same shape as r.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.basic.EnergyRBFEmbedding(e_min, e_max, n_rbf)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Gaussian RBF embedding for scalar energy grid indices.

Parameters:

  • e_min (float) – Minimum grid index (left edge of RBF center grid).

  • e_max (float) – Maximum grid index (right edge of RBF center grid).

  • n_rbf (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

__init__(e_min, e_max, n_rbf)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyRBFEmbedding.

forward(energies)[source]¶

Embed energy grid indices as Gaussian RBF features.

Parameters:

energies (Tensor) – Energy grid indices of shape (nE,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

RBF features of shape (nE, n_rbf).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.basic.MLP(in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim, n_layers=2, dropout=0.0, layer_norm=False)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Simple multi-layer perceptron with SiLU activations.

Parameters:

  • in_dim (int) – Size of the input features.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Size of each hidden layer.

  • out_dim (int) – Size of the output features.

  • n_layers (int) – Total number of linear layers (including the output layer).

  • dropout (float) – Dropout probability applied after each hidden activation. Set to 0.0 to disable.

  • layer_norm (bool) – If True, apply LayerNorm after each hidden linear layer.

__init__(in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim, n_layers=2, dropout=0.0, layer_norm=False)[source]¶

Initialize MLP.

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply the MLP.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input tensor of shape (..., in_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Output tensor of shape (..., out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.basic.RadialMLP(in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Two-hidden-layer MLP for radial (TP) weight prediction.

Parameters:

  • in_dim (int) – Input size (typically RBF dimension plus optional extras).

  • hidden_dim (int) – Size of each hidden layer.

  • out_dim (int) – Output size (typically the number of TP weights).

__init__(in_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize RadialMLP.

forward(x)[source]¶

Predict radial weights.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input features of shape (..., in_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Predicted weights of shape (..., out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.basic.IrrepNorm(irreps, eps=1e-08, affine=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Irrep-respecting normalization.

For l = 0 blocks the multiplicity channels are mean/variance normalized. For l > 0 blocks each irrep copy is RMS-normalized across its irrep dimension.

Parameters:

  • irreps (Irreps) – The irreps specification that describes the feature layout.

  • eps (float) – Small constant added to denominators for numerical stability.

  • affine (bool) – If True, learn per-channel scale and bias parameters.

__init__(irreps, eps=1e-08, affine=True)[source]¶

Initialize IrrepNorm.

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply irrep-respecting normalization.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input features of shape (..., irreps.dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Normalized features with the same shape as x.

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_absorber module¶

Invariant absorber branch for E3EE.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_absorber.EnergyConditionedAbsorberBranch(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-dependent absorber branch based on invariant absorber features.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of the invariant absorber feature vector.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the MLP.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedAbsorberBranch.

forward(h_abs, e_feat)[source]¶

Compute absorber branch latent from absorber features and energy embedding.

Parameters:

  • h_abs (Tensor) – Absorber invariant features, shape (B, H).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_attention module¶

Energy-conditioned invariant atom-attention branch for E3EE.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_attention.EnergyConditionedAtomAttention(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned attention over invariant atomwise features.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of the invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension; must be divisible by n_heads.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radius of the attention neighborhood graph in Angstrom.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for the absorber->atom distance.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • n_heads (int) – Number of attention heads.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedAtomAttention.

forward(h, z, mask, e_feat, absorber_index, att_dst, att_dist)[source]¶

Compute energy-conditioned attention over atoms and return latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask (encoder scope), shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber index per sample, shape (B,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N (attention scope), shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom distances in Angstrom, shape (E_att,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_convolution module¶

Invariant and equivariant convolution branches for the E3EE model.

Provides SchNet/PaiNN-style invariant convolution (EnergyConditionedAtomConvolution) and a NequIP/MACE-style equivariant counterpart (EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomConvolution). Energy conditioning is applied after aggregation to avoid materialising a per-edge energy axis, which significantly reduces activation memory.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_convolution.EnergyConditionedAtomConvolution(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Invariant SchNet/PaiNN-style convolution producing a per-(absorber, energy) latent.

For each sample’s absorber:

m_{abs<-j} = MLP(h_j, z_j, RBF(dist), is_abs) # SchNet message m_{abs<-j} *= cos_envelope(dist) [optional] m_{abs<-j} *= sigmoid(MLP(h_abs, h_j, RBF)) # PaiNN gate m_abs = sum_j (j in att-graph) # sum aggregation out_abs[e] = MLP(EnergyMod_scalar(m_abs, e_feat)) # energy mod

Energy conditioning runs after aggregation, so per-edge tensors carry no nE axis (large activation-memory saving).

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radius of the attention neighborhood graph in Angstrom.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for the absorber->atom distance.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style learned scalar edge gate.

__init__(atom_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedAtomConvolution.

forward(h, z, mask, e_feat, absorber_index, att_dst, att_dist)[source]¶

Compute invariant convolution branch latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features, shape (B, N, H).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber index per sample, shape (B,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N (absorber’s neighbors), shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom distances in Angstrom, shape (E_att,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_convolution.EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomConvolution(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

NequIP/MACE-style equivariant convolution producing a per-(absorber, energy) latent.

For each sample’s absorber:

sh_j = Y(u_{abs->j}) v_{abs<-j} = TP(h_full[j], sh_j; W(z_j, RBF, is_abs)) # NequIP message v_{abs<-j} *= cos_envelope(dist) [optional] v_{abs<-j} *= sigmoid(MLP(h_abs, h_j, RBF)) # PaiNN gate v_abs = sum_j v_{abs<-j} # sum agg v_{abs,e} = EnergyIrrepModulation(v_abs, e_feat) # energy mod out = MLP(invariants(v_{abs,e}))

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the full equivariant atom features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radius of the attention neighborhood graph in Angstrom.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for bond directions.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Target irreps of the equivariant message (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o").

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for the absorber->atom distance.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style learned scalar edge gate.

__init__(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, use_gate=True)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomConvolution.

forward(h, h_full, z, mask, e_feat, absorber_index, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec)[source]¶

Apply equivariant convolution and return per-(absorber, energy) latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (B, N, H).

  • h_full (Tensor) – Full equivariant atom features (B, N, irreps_node.dim).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF embedding (nE, e_dim).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber index per sample (B,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N for the attention neighborhood (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom distances (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom displacement vectors (E_att, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_eq_attention module¶

Energy-conditioned equivariant atom-attention branch for E3EE.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_eq_attention.EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomAttention(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Equivariant counterpart of EnergyConditionedAtomAttention.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features (used for scoring).

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the full equivariant atom features (TP input).

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of all internal MLPs.

  • latent_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension; must be divisible by n_heads.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radius of the attention neighborhood graph in Angstrom.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for bond directions.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Target irreps of the equivariant values (e.g. "32x0e+16x1o").

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for the absorber->atom distance.

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • n_heads (int) – Number of attention heads used for invariant scoring.

__init__(atom_dim, irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, latent_dim, att_cutoff, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, rbf_dim=16, max_z=100, z_emb_dim=32, n_heads=4)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedEquivariantAtomAttention.

forward(h, h_full, z, mask, e_feat, absorber_index, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec)[source]¶

Compute equivariant energy-conditioned attention over atoms and return latent.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (used for scoring), shape (B, N, H).

  • h_full (Tensor) – Full equivariant atom features (TP input for values), shape (B, N, irreps_node.dim).

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, dE).

  • absorber_index (Tensor) – Absorber index per sample, shape (B,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom distances in Angstrom, shape (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Absorber-to-atom displacement vectors in Angstrom, shape (E_att, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, latent_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_equivariant module¶

Equivariant absorber head and energy-irrep modulation for E3EE.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_equivariant.EnergyConditionedEquivariantAbsorberHead(irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Late equivariant absorber head.

Applies energy-conditioned irrep-wise modulation to the absorber equivariant feature, converts to invariants, then projects.

Parameters:

  • irreps_node (Irreps) – Irreps of the absorber equivariant features.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension used in the modulation and output MLPs.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

__init__(irreps_node, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyConditionedEquivariantAbsorberHead.

forward(h_abs_full, e_feat)[source]¶

Extract invariants from absorber equivariant features and project to latent.

Parameters:

  • h_abs_full (Tensor) – Equivariant absorber features, shape (B, D).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_equivariant.EnergyIrrepModulation(irreps, e_dim, hidden_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned scalar modulation of each irrep copy, preserving equivariance.

A scalar gate per irrep copy is predicted from the energy embedding and applied multiplicatively to all dim-dimensional components of that copy. Because a scalar multiplier commutes with rotations, equivariance is preserved.

Parameters:

  • irreps (Irreps) – The irreps layout of the features to be modulated.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding (MLP input).

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the gate MLP.

__init__(irreps, e_dim, hidden_dim)[source]¶

Initialize EnergyIrrepModulation.

forward(x, e_feat)[source]¶

Apply per-copy scalar energy modulation to equivariant features.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Equivariant features, shape (B, D).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Modulated equivariant features of shape (B, nE, D).

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_fusion module¶

Branch fusion layers for E3EE.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_fusion.GatedBranchFusion(branch_dims, fused_dim, cond_dim, hidden_dim, use_softmax=True)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned gated fusion of active branch latents.

Each active branch is projected to fused_dim and combined with a learned soft gate predicted from the projected branch values, their magnitudes, and a conditioning feature such as the energy embedding.

Parameters:

  • branch_dims (list[int]) – Feature dimensions of the active branch tensors.

  • fused_dim (int) – Common latent dimension used for gated summation.

  • cond_dim (int) – Dimension of the conditioning features.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the gate and output MLPs.

  • use_softmax (bool) – If True, normalize gates with softmax. Otherwise, use sigmoid gates normalized to sum to one.

__init__(branch_dims, fused_dim, cond_dim, hidden_dim, use_softmax=True)[source]¶

Initialize GatedBranchFusion.

reset_gate_logits()[source]¶

Initialize the final gate layer to produce uniform gates.

Return type:

None

forward(branches, cond_feat, return_gates=False)[source]¶

Fuse active branch tensors.

Parameters:

  • branches (list[Tensor]) – Active branch tensors with matching leading dimensions. Each tensor has shape (..., branch_dim_i).

  • cond_feat (Tensor) – Conditioning features of shape (..., cond_dim) or any trailing-leading shape broadcastable to the branches, such as (nE, cond_dim) for branch tensors shaped (B, nE, D).

  • return_gates (bool) – If True, return the normalized gate weights along with the fused tensor.

Return type:

Tensor | tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

Returns:

Fused tensor of shape (..., fused_dim). If return_gates is True, returns (fused, gates) where gates has shape (..., n_branches).

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_path module¶

3-body path aggregation branch for E3EE.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_path.PairElementEnergyScattering(max_z, z_emb_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Energy-conditioned element-pair scattering features.

Operates on flat per-path tensors (P, ...) rather than padded (B, n_paths, ...).

Parameters:

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by the element embedding.

  • z_emb_dim (int) – Embedding dimension for atomic numbers.

  • e_dim (int) – Dimension of the energy RBF embedding.

  • hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the output MLP.

  • out_dim (int) – Output feature dimension per path.

__init__(max_z, z_emb_dim, e_dim, hidden_dim, out_dim)[source]¶

Initialize PairElementEnergyScattering.

forward(z_j, z_k, e_feat)[source]¶

Compute per-path element-pair energy features.

Parameters:

  • z_j (Tensor) – Atomic numbers of the j leg, shape (P,).

  • z_k (Tensor) – Atomic numbers of the k leg, shape (P,).

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-path energy features of shape (P, nE, out_dim).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.branch_path.AbsorberPathAggregator(atom_dim, rbf_dim, geom_hidden_dim, scatter_dim, out_dim, cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: Module

3-body absorber-centred path aggregator for paths (absorber, j, k).

Consumes precomputed flat triplet scalars (r0j, r0k, rjk, cos(angle)) and per-path flat atom indices into the padded B * N node layout, together with path_batch for scatter-aggregating into the batch dimension.

Parameters:

  • atom_dim (int) – Dimension of invariant per-atom features.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for distance encoding.

  • geom_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the geometry MLP.

  • scatter_dim (int) – Intermediate scatter feature dimension.

  • out_dim (int) – Output (latent) dimension.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff used for the cosine envelope weights.

__init__(atom_dim, rbf_dim, geom_hidden_dim, scatter_dim, out_dim, cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize AbsorberPathAggregator.

forward(h_flat, z_flat, pair_elem_energy, e_feat, path_j, path_k, path_r0j, path_r0k, path_rjk, path_cosangle, path_batch, bsz)[source]¶

Aggregate path contributions into per-(sample, energy) latent vectors.

Parameters:

  • h_flat (Tensor) – Invariant atom features (flattened), shape (B*N, atom_dim).

  • z_flat (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (flattened), shape (B*N,).

  • pair_elem_energy (PairElementEnergyScattering) – Pre-built element-pair energy module.

  • e_feat (Tensor) – Energy RBF features, shape (nE, e_dim).

  • path_j (Tensor) – Flat atom indices for leg j (into B*N), shape (P,).

  • path_k (Tensor) – Flat atom indices for leg k (into B*N), shape (P,).

  • path_r0j (Tensor) – Absorber-to-j distance in Angstrom, shape (P,).

  • path_r0k (Tensor) – Absorber-to-k distance in Angstrom, shape (P,).

  • path_rjk (Tensor) – j-to-k distance in Angstrom, shape (P,).

  • path_cosangle (Tensor) – Cosine of the j-absorber-k angle, shape (P,).

  • path_batch (Tensor) – Batch index per path (0..B-1), shape (P,).

  • bsz (int) – Batch size.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Latent tensor of shape (B, nE, out_dim).

xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.interactions module¶

Equivariant message-passing interaction block for E3EE.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee.layers.interactions.EquivariantInteractionBlock(irreps_node, irreps_sh, irreps_message, rbf_dim, radial_hidden_dim, cutoff, residual_scale_init=0.1)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Single equivariant message-passing interaction block using e3nn tensor products with gating.

Parameters:

  • irreps_node (str) – Irreps of the per-atom node features (input and output).

  • irreps_sh (str) – Irreps of the spherical harmonics on each edge.

  • irreps_message (str) – Intermediate irreps of the message before gating.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF features encoding edge lengths.

  • radial_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the radial weight MLP.

  • cutoff (float) – Smooth cutoff radius; edges beyond this have zero weight.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial value for the learnable residual scale.

__init__(irreps_node, irreps_sh, irreps_message, rbf_dim, radial_hidden_dim, cutoff, residual_scale_init=0.1)[source]¶

Initialize EquivariantInteractionBlock.

forward(x, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_sh, edge_rbf, edge_len)[source]¶

Apply one equivariant message-passing step.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Node features of shape (B*N, irreps_node.dim).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Source flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Destination flat indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_sh (Tensor) – Spherical harmonics on each edge, shape (E, irreps_sh.dim).

  • edge_rbf (Tensor) – RBF-encoded edge lengths, shape (E, rbf_dim).

  • edge_len (Tensor) – Raw edge lengths in Angstrom, shape (E,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated node features of shape (B*N, irreps_node.dim).
